task.name: JSON Schema Validator
task.description: Validates a JSON artifact against its registered project schema. Reports violations and optionally auto-fixes them and injects the $schema field. Use when the user asks to validate, check, or verify a JSON artifact, or after writing any JSON output.
task.objective: Validate a JSON artifact against its schema and report (or fix) any violations

input.name: content
input.desc: The JSON artifact to validate — provide either a file path or raw JSON text

input.name: schema_path
input.desc: Override the auto-resolved schema path. Use when the artifact type cannot be inferred or when a non-standard schema applies.

input.name: mode
input.desc: 'report' = list all violations and warnings only (default). 'fix' = apply violations as corrections and output the corrected JSON, then inject $schema field if missing.

llm.i: MANDATORY: Execute ALL steps in the flow section IN EXACT ORDER
DO NOT skip steps or change the sequence
HALT immediately when halt-conditions are met
Each action xml tag within step xml tag is a REQUIRED action to complete that step
Sections outside flow (validation, output-format, halt-conditions) provide essential context — review and apply throughout execution

step.title: Load Content
step.action: If content is a file path, read the file contents; otherwise treat content as raw JSON
Parse the JSON to confirm it is syntactically valid
HALT with error: "Invalid JSON — fix syntax errors before running validation" and show the parse error location
Note the filename (or infer a placeholder like "inline-artifact") for the report header

step.title: Resolve Schema
step.action: If schema_path input was provided, use it directly — skip auto-detection
Otherwise, check if the artifact contains a "$schema" field; if so, resolve that path relative to the artifact's location and use it
Otherwise, match the filename against the schema-registry entries above (longest/most-specific pattern wins)
HALT and ask the user to supply the schema_path input, listing all available patterns from the schema-registry
Confirm the resolved schema file exists on disk before proceeding
HALT with error: "Schema file not found at [path] — verify {aac-path} resolves correctly in this context"

step.title: Read Schema
step.action: Read the full schema JSON file
Extract: required fields list, property types, enum values, nested object shapes, array item schemas, and any $ref references that need resolving
Follow and inline any $ref references within the schema before validation

step.title: Validate
step.action: Check all top-level required fields are present and non-empty (null, "", or [] counts as missing unless the schema explicitly allows it)
For each property in the artifact, verify its type matches the schema type
For enum fields, verify the value is in the schema's enum array; flag unknowns as violations
For object fields (e.g. userStory), verify the expected sub-keys are present (e.g. asA / iWant / soThat — never a plain string)
For array fields, verify each item conforms to the array item schema
For acceptanceCriteria items, verify each has at minimum: given, when, then — not just IDs or counts
Collect every violation with: JSON path, issue description, expected value/type, actual value/type
Collect warnings for: missing optional-but-recommended fields, fields present in schema description but absent in artifact, deprecated field names

step.title: Output Results
step.action: Render the findings using the output-format below
Apply each violation as a correction to the artifact JSON
Inject "$schema" as the very first key, using a relative path from the artifact's location to the resolved schema file. DO NOT modify an existing $schema field.
Output the complete corrected JSON in a fenced code block
Note: $schema injection is this task's responsibility only when mode=fix. For Markdown→JSON conversion, use the dual-output-json task instead.

validation.i: userStory MUST be an object with asA / iWant / soThat keys — never a plain string
acceptanceCriteria items MUST have given / when / then — IDs or counts alone are violations
Enum fields: compare value against schema enum array; any value not in the array is a violation
Required fields that are null, "", or [] are treated as missing unless schema explicitly allows it
$schema field: only inject if missing AND mode=fix; never remove or modify an existing $schema
$schema relative path: calculate from the artifact file's directory to the schema file
Do not silently skip unresolvable $ref entries in the schema — flag them as warnings

output-format.example:
## Schema Validation Report **Artifact:** story-4.1.json **Schema:** schemas/bmm/story.schema.json **Result:** INVALID ❌ (3 violations, 1 warning)
### Violations (3) | # | JSON Path | Issue | Expected | Actual | |---|---|---|---|---| | 1 | $.metadata.status | Invalid enum value | "draft" \| "active" \| "completed" \| "archived" | "unknown" | | 2 | $.userStory | Must be an object, not a string | { asA, iWant, soThat } | "As a user..." | | 3 | $.acceptanceCriteria[0] | Missing required field | "given" | (absent) |
### Warnings (1) | # | JSON Path | Recommendation | |---|---|---| | 1 | $.implementationNotes | Optional field present in schema but absent — consider adding |
## Schema Validation Report **Artifact:** prd.json **Schema:** schemas/bmm/prd.schema.json **Result:** VALID ✅ (0 violations, 0 warnings)

halt-conditions.condition: HALT if content is empty or not provided
HALT if JSON is syntactically invalid — show parse error; do not attempt schema validation
HALT if no schema can be resolved and no schema_path override was given
HALT if the resolved schema file does not exist on disk
